      • initRouterErrorHandlers()

Imports

request, response, autolink

Types

Afterware = Middleware
Callable = (when defined supraMicroservice:
  proc (req: ptr Request; res: ptr Response): void {.nimcall, ...gcsafe.}
 else:
  proc (req: var Request; res: var Response): void {.nimcall, ...gcsafe.})
HttpRoute = ref object of RootObj
  callback*: Callable
  case
  of DynamicRoute:
  else:
    nil
  case
  of true:
  else:
    nil
  case
  of true:
  else:
    nil
HttpRouterError = object of CatchableError
HttpRouterInstance = object
  httpGet*, httpPost*, httpPut*, httpHead*, httpConnect*, httpDelete*,
  httpPatch*, httpTrace*, httpOptions*, httpErrors*: CritBitTree[HttpRoute]
  httpWS*: CritBitTree[HttpRouteWs]
HttpRouteType = enum
  StaticRoute, DynamicRoute
HttpRouteWs = ref object of HttpRoute
Middleware = proc (req: var Request; res: var Response): HttpCode {.nimcall.}

Vars

baseMiddlewares {.compileTime.} = CacheTable("BaseMiddlewares")
queuedRoutes {.compileTime.} = CacheTable("QueuedRoutes")

Consts

DefaultHttpError: Callable = :anonymous_3841982540
httpMethods = ["get", "post", "put", "patch", "head", "delete", "trace",
               "options", "connect", "ws"]

Procs

proc call4xx(router: var HttpRouterInstance; req: var Request; res: var Response) {.
    discardable, ...raises: [Exception, KeyError], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Run the 4xx callback 
proc checkExists(router: var HttpRouterInstance; requestPath: string;
                 httpMethod: HttpMethod): tuple[exists: bool, route: HttpRoute,
    params: TableRef[string, string]] {....raises: [KeyError, ValueError,
    RegexInternalError, InvalidUnicodeError, SyntaxError, StudyError], tags: [],
                                        forbids: [].}
proc checkWsExists(router: var HttpRouterInstance; requestPath: string): tuple[
    exists: bool, route: HttpRouteWs, params: TableRef[string, string]] {....raises: [
    KeyError, ValueError, RegexInternalError, InvalidUnicodeError, SyntaxError,
    StudyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc errorHandler(router: var HttpRouterInstance; code: HttpCode;
                  callback: Callable) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc newHttpRouter(): HttpRouterInstance {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc parseRouteNode(verb, routePath: string;
                    middlewares, afterwares: var NimNode;
                    closureHandle: NimNode = nil) {.compileTime,
    ...raises: [ValueError, KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc preparePath(path: string; prefix = ""): string {.compileTime, ...raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc registerRoute(router: var HttpRouterInstance;
                   autolinked: sink (string, string); httpMethod: HttpMethod;
                   callback: Callable; middlewares: seq[Middleware] = @[];
                   afterwares: seq[Afterware] = @[]; isWebSocket = false) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a new Route 
proc resolveAfterware(route: HttpRoute; req: var Request; res: var Response): HttpCode {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Resolve an Afterware handle after a request has been made 
proc resolveMiddleware(route: HttpRoute; req: var Request; res: var Response): HttpCode {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Checks a route if has any implemented middlewares

Macros

macro routes(body: untyped): untyped

Register routes at compile-time under the routes macro. Routes are auto-linked to their controller based on given path. For example a route like get "/electronics/{category:id}" will try to link to getElectronicsCategory handle

routes:
get "/auth/login"
post "/auth/login"

get "/auth/register"
post "/auth/register" {.afterware: @[newCustomerEvent].}

# attach one or more middlwares to the route.
# note that middlewares are called in the given order
get "/account" {.middleware: @[auth, member].}
This macro supports groups of routes (under the same prefix). Also, grouped routes can be middleware-protected:

group "/account":
{.middleware: authenticated.}:
get "/"         # GET /account
get "/profile"  # GET /account/profile
post "/profile" # POST /account/profile

macro searchRouteWs()
Search for a WebSocket route

Templates

template initRouterErrorHandlers()
